<treqs-element type="Type_of_Threat" id="x0400000000003ADD">

Threats to vehicles regarding their communication channels - 4.3.2 Threats to vehicles regarding their communication channels
- **xid**: x0400000000003ADD
- **sid**: SI0194

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000003ADF">

Security policies - 3.1.1. Guidance related to security policies specified in ISO/SAE 21434 may apply. 
3.1.2. The following points may also apply:
• Policies for cybersecurity shall be defined and approved by management and communicated to employees
• Policies to be reviewed at planned intervals or when significant changes occur to ensure their suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
- **xid**: x0400000000003ADF
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Stakeholder_Req" id="x0400000000003AE2">

Compliance - The system shall adhere to international anti-theft standards and all the applicable legal compliance in all the regions.
- **xid**: x0400000000003AE2
- **sid**: SI0002

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Baseline_Architecture" id="x0400000000003AE5">

Immobilizer (IMMO) - System design of an AUTOSAR-compliant Immobilizer Manager

The immobilizer (IMMO) system in a modern car represents the anti-theft system, protecting the vehicle from unauthorized driving. Immobilizer receives the driver’s intentions, senses the environment and controls the behavior of immobilizer sources in the vehicle. In case of any attempt for unauthorized engine start, immobilizer requests for visual and acoustical alarms to be generated. All these decisions are taken based on the status of user provided key authorization.

The core functionality of IMMO is implemented within Immobilizer Manager (ImobMgr) SW-C. It incorporates the basic functionalities like key learning, learnt key verification, IMMO diagnostics, alarm, etc. The data shared between these functionalities is encrypted using AUTOSAR (AUTomotive Open System ARchitecture) encryption service to ensure security operation.

An AUTOSAR compliant IMMO ECU involves both hardware and software components, with a structured approach to functionality and communication. It contains multiple software components (SW-Cs), a variety of ports and interfaces, and several AUTOSAR services for secure communication, diagnostics, and system management. Let's dive deeper into these details, breaking down each part of an AUTOSAR compliant IMMO ECU.
Components of an Immobilizer System
An IMMO system involves a network of ECUs, each running multiple software components that interact with each other. These components are responsible for handling tasks like secure communication, engine start authorization, diagnostics, and monitoring.

                                                           Figure 1: Immobilizer Decomposition
- **xid**: x0400000000003AE5
- **sid**: SI0122

<treqs-element type="Stakeholder_Req" id="x0400000000037445">

Anti theft system - The vehicle's engine immobilizer system, compliant with UNECE R155 and ISO 21434, shall prevent unauthorized engine cranking by employing cryptographic key authentication and secure communication protocols within an AUTOSAR-compliant architecture, meeting ASPICE process maturity level 2 or higher.
- **xid**: x0400000000037445
- **sid**: SI0002

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Goal" id="x0400000000038441">

Secure CAN communication - AUTOSAR FO R20-11

Authentication and integrity protection of sensitive data is necessary to protect correct and safe functionality of the vehicle systems - this ensures that received data comes from the right ECU and has the correct value.
The SecOC module as described in this document provides functionality necessary to verify the authenticity and freshness of PDU based communication between ECUs
within the vehicle architecture. The approach requires both the sending ECU and the receiving ECU to implement a SecOC module.
To provide message freshness, the SecOC module on the sending and receiving side get freshness from an external Freshness Manager for each uniquely identifiable Secured I-PDU, i.e. for each secured communication link.

On the sender side, the SecOC module creates a Secured I-PDU by adding authentication information to the outgoing Authentic I-PDU. The authentication information comprises of an Authenticator (e.g. Message Authentication Code) and optionally a Freshness Value. Regardless if the Freshness Value is or is not included in
the Secure I-PDU payload, the Freshness Value is considered during generation of the Authenticator. When using a Freshness Counter instead of a Timestamp, the Freshness Counter should be incremented by the Freshness Manager prior to providing the authentication information to the receiver side. On the receiver side, the SecOC  module checks the freshness and authenticity of the Authentic I-PDU by verifying the authentication information that has been appended by the sending side SecOC module. To verify the authenticity and freshness of an Authentic I-PDU, the Secured I-PDU provided to the receiving side SecOC should be the same Secured I-PDU provided by the sending side SecOC and the receiving side SecOC should have knowledge of the Freshness Value used by the sending side SecOC during creation of the Authenticator.
- **xid**: x0400000000038441
- **sid**: SI0166
<treqs-link type="On_Threat_Scenario" target="x0400000000038478"/>
<treqs-link type="Supported_by_Cybersecurity_Requirement" target="x0400000000038CD8"/>
<treqs-link type="Based_on_Mitigation_from_Catalog" target="x0400000000038CE3"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Threat_Scenario" id="x0400000000038478">

- **xid**: x0400000000038478
- **sid**: SI0172

<treqs-element type="Threat_Scenario" id="x040000000003847E">

Compromised IMMO key ID - Cloning of the Key: The IMMO key could have been cloned using specialized tools or software, allowing a duplicate key to be created. This can happen if an attacker has physical access to the key or the key's signal is intercepted.

Security Vulnerabilities in the System: If there is a flaw in the vehicle's IMMO system or in its communication protocol (e.g., weak encryption, predictable algorithms), it could have been exploited by an attacker to gain unauthorized access.

Key Storage Vulnerabilities: If the vehicle's key data is stored insecurely, such as in an unprotected database or improperly encrypted form, this could be another point of vulnerability that allows an attacker to steal the key.

Immediate actions are required to mitigate the risks associated with this compromise. These include:

    Revocation and Replacement of Compromised Keys: All compromised keys should be revoked, and new ones should be programmed to restore security.

    System Audit and Patching: A thorough audit of the IMMO system should be conducted to identify potential vulnerabilities and address them through software updates or hardware upgrades.

    Enhanced Security Measures: Consider upgrading the IMMO system to a higher level of security, such as implementing more robust encryption or introducing additional authentication layers.
- **xid**: x040000000003847E
- **sid**: SI0172
<treqs-link type="Leads_to_Damage_Scenario" target="x0400000000038460"/>
<treqs-link type="Attack_Path" target="x0400000000038DA4"/>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Claim" id="x040000000003848E">

Secure IMMO function is secure enough - Potential Impact of a Compromised IMMO System:

    Vehicle Theft: The primary function of the IMMO system is to prevent unauthorized starting of the vehicle. A compromised IMMO system means that thieves could bypass the security measures and start the vehicle without the legitimate key, leading to increased theft risk.

    Loss of Control: A compromised IMMO system could allow an attacker to take control of key functions of the vehicle. In some cases, attackers could disable the immobilizer entirely or manipulate other systems to make the vehicle inoperable, posing a serious risk to both the vehicle owner and others on the road.

    Reputation and Liability: For manufacturers and service providers, the compromise of the IMMO system could lead to reputational damage, loss of consumer trust, and potential legal liability in the case of theft or accidents caused by security vulnerabilities.
- **xid**: x040000000003848E
- **sid**: SI0426
<treqs-link type="On_Threat_Scenario" target="x040000000003847E"/>
<treqs-link type="Supported_by_Cybersecurity_Requirement" target="x0400000000038D25"/>
<treqs-link type="Based_on_Mitigation_from_Catalog" target="x0400000000038DD9"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x04000000000384CF">

UNECE Regulation No.155 - The system shall implement cybersecurity measures to prevent and detect threats affecting vehicle systems.
- **xid**: x04000000000384CF
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x04000000000384D4">

ISO/SAE 21434 - The system shall be developed using a structured approach to manage cybersecurity risks throughout the lifecycle.
- **xid**: x04000000000384D4
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x04000000000384D8">

ISO 26262 - The system shall manage functional safety requirements related to hazard analysis and risk assessment.
- **xid**: x04000000000384D8
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x04000000000384DC">

FMVSS No.114 - The vehicle shall prevent engine start without key authentication and shall secue against unauthorized movement.
- **xid**: x04000000000384DC
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement_Group" id="x04000000000384E0">

Anti theft system - The immobilizer shall prevent unauthorized engine start.
- **xid**: x04000000000384E0
- **sid**: SI0472
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x04000000000384E2"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x04000000000384E6"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x04000000000384EA"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x04000000000384E2">

Engine start/stop - The immobilizer system shall control the engine start and stop functionality based on authentication of a valid key. If valid key is detected, the system shall allow engine to start. If the key is unauthorized or missing, the system shall prevent start to ensure vehicle security.
- **xid**: x04000000000384E2
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x04000000000384E6">

Engine start time - The immobilizer system shall ensure the engine start within a predefined time limit after valid key is authenticated.
- **xid**: x04000000000384E6
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x04000000000384EA">

Compliance for anti theft system - The system shall comply with international regulations for engine immobilziation, locking mechanisms and vehicle security standards.
- **xid**: x04000000000384EA
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement_Group" id="x04000000000384EE">

Anti theft system - The immobilizer shall prevent unauthorized engine start.
- **xid**: x04000000000384EE
- **sid**: SI0472
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x04000000000384E6"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x0400000000038E9F"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x040000000003A013"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement_Group" id="x04000000000384F4">

Key management - The immobilizer shall authenticate the user's key before allowing engine start.
- **xid**: x04000000000384F4
- **sid**: SI0472
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x0400000000038EA2"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x0400000000038EA6"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x0400000000038EAA"/>
<treqs-link type="Function_Requirement" target="x0400000000038EAE"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Requirement" id="x0400000000038CD8">

SecOC - Secure On-board Communication:
Authentication and integrity protection of sensitive data is necessary to protect correct and safe functionality of the vehicle systems - this ensures that received data comes from the right ECU and has the correct value.
The SecOC module as described in this document provides functionality necessary to verify the authenticity and freshness of PDU based communication between ECUs
within the vehicle architecture. The approach requires both the sending ECU and the receiving ECU to implement a SecOC module.
To provide message freshness, the SecOC module on the sending and receiving side get freshness from an external Freshness Manager for each uniquely identifiable Secured I-PDU, i.e. for each secured communication link.

On the sender side, the SecOC module creates a Secured I-PDU by adding authentication information to the outgoing Authentic I-PDU. The authentication information comprises of an Authenticator (e.g. Message Authentication Code) and optionally a Freshness Value. Regardless if the Freshness Value is or is not included in
the Secure I-PDU payload, the Freshness Value is considered during generation of the Authenticator. When using a Freshness Counter instead of a Timestamp, the Freshness Counter should be incremented by the Freshness Manager prior to providing the authentication information to the receiver side. On the receiver side, the SecOC  module checks the freshness and authenticity of the Authentic I-PDU by verifying the authentication information that has been appended by the sending side SecOC module. To verify the authenticity and freshness of an Authentic I-PDU, the Secured I-PDU provided to the receiving side SecOC should be the same Secured I-PDU provided by the sending side SecOC and the receiving side SecOC should have knowledge of the Freshness Value used by the sending side SecOC during creation of the Authenticator.
- **xid**: x0400000000038CD8
- **sid**: SI0187

</treqs-element>

Intrusion detection for CAN bus anomalies
- **xid**: x0400000000038CE3
- **sid**: SI0222

<treqs-element type="Abstract_Interface" id="x0400000000038D03">

Ignition - Ignitition modes: (taken from Internet)

0
The odom eter, clock and temperature gauge are illuminated1.
 The power seats can be adjusted.
 The power windows can be used.
 The infotainment system can be used.
In this mode, the functions are available for a limited time and then switch off automatically.

I
Power windows,
Bluetooth,
Navigation,
Phone,
Blower and Windshield wipers can be used.
Electrical current will be taken from the battery in this ignition mode.


II
The headlights illuminate.
Warning/indicator lights illuminate for limited time
This ignition mode uses a lot of current from the battery and should be avoided whenever possible!
- **xid**: x0400000000038D03
- **sid**: SI0230

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038E9F">

Compliance for anti theft system - The system shall comply with international regulations for engine immobilziation, locking mechanisms and vehicle security standards.
- **xid**: x0400000000038E9F
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EA2">

Key encryption and decryption - The system shall perform cryptographic verification (e.g., encryption and decryption) between the key fob and vehicle to ensure authenticity.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EA2
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EA6">

Key managment - The system shall support the key management services such as generating, storing and sharing the keys in a secure manner.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EA6
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EAA">

Compliance for key management - The system shall comply with encryption standards and secure key authentication protocols to prevent unauthorized duplication or access.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EAA
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EAE">

Keyless entry - The system shall support keyless entry and engine start when a valid key fob is within a specified range.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EAE
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EB6">

Diagnostic communication manager - The system shall provide diagnostic information for service technicians, such as error codes for failed authentication or key detection issues.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EB6
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EBA">

Authentication time - The system should be quick enough to detect and authenticate key. It should be quick enough for the user having valid key fob.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EBA
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x0400000000038EBE">

Compliance for unauthorized start detection - The system shall adhere to legal requirements for alarm activation, intrusion detection and diagnostic reporting of unauthorized access attempts.
- **xid**: x0400000000038EBE
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Interface" id="x0400000000038EC4">

- **xid**: x0400000000038EC4
- **sid**: SI0477

</treqs-element>

EncryptedKeyData
- **xid**: x0400000000038ED3
- **sid**: SI0477

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement_Group" id="x0400000000038ED7">

Unauthorized start detection - The immobilizer system shall detect unauthorized engine start attempts and trigger alarms.
- **xid**: x0400000000038ED7
- **sid**: SI0472

- **sid**: SI0011

- **sid**: SI0125

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x040000000003926F">

Key authentication before engine start - If the key authentication response is not received within N ms, then ImobMgr send a "Blocked" signal to the Engine Control Module (ECM).
- **xid**: x040000000003926F
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039273">

Engine stop on key removal - If the key is removed or undetectable for more than N seconds, then ImobMgr shall send an engine stop request to Engine Control Module (ECM).
- **xid**: x0400000000039273
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039278">

Ensure timely engine start - If the key authentication process successful, then ImbobMgr send engine start signal to ECM within N time.
- **xid**: x0400000000039278
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x040000000003927C">

Apply engine start timeout - If authentication process exceeds predefined timeout, then ImobMgr send an engine start block signal to ECM.
- **xid**: x040000000003927C
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039281">

Trigger alarm for unauthorized start - If an unauthorized start attempt is detected, then ImobMgr send an alarm activation signal to HMI, ATWS and Horn control.
- **xid**: x0400000000039281
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039285">

Activate acoustic alarm - If unauthorized start attempt is detected, then ImobMgr send "AlrmAcoustCmd1" to Horn Control.
- **xid**: x0400000000039285
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x040000000003928E">

Limit authentication time - If authentication takes longer than specified N threshold, then ImobMgr log a delay warning for diagnostics.
- **xid**: x040000000003928E
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039293">

Regulatory compliance for unauthorized start detection - If ImobMgr detects an unauthorized engine start attempt, then ImobMgr ensures compliance with international standard requirements for alarm activation, intrusion detection and diagnostoc reporting.
- **xid**: x0400000000039293
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Generic_Threat" id="x0400000000039323">

Software or hardware development permits vulnerabilities - Software or hardware development permits vulnerabilities
- **xid**: x0400000000039323
- **sid**: SI0164

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Generic_Threat" id="x0400000000039327">

Network design introduces vulnerabilities - Network design introduces vulnerabilities
- **xid**: x0400000000039327
- **sid**: SI0164
<treqs-link type="Type_of_Threat" target="x040000000003931C"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x040000000003932B">

Denial of service, for example this may be triggered on the internal network by flooding a CAN bus,… - Denial of service, for example this may be triggered on the internal network by flooding a CAN bus, or by provoking faults on an ECU via a high rate of messaging
- **xid**: x040000000003932B
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039315"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x040000000003932F">

Combination of short encryption keys and long period of validity enables attacker to break encrypti… - Combination of short encryption keys and long period of validity enables attacker to break encryption
- **xid**: x040000000003932F
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039319"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x0400000000039333">

Insufficient use of cryptographic algorithms to protect sensitive systems - Insufficient use of cryptographic algorithms to protect sensitive systems
- **xid**: x0400000000039333
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039319"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x0400000000039337">

Using already or soon to be deprecated cryptographic algorithms - Using already or soon to be deprecated cryptographic algorithms
- **xid**: x0400000000039337
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039319"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x040000000003933B">

Hardware or software, engineered to enable an attack or fails to meet design criteria to stop an at… - Hardware or software, engineered to enable an attack or fails to meet design criteria to stop an attack
- **xid**: x040000000003933B
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Asset_Vulnerability_to_Attack" target="x0400000000039305"/>
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x040000000003931F"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x0400000000039340">

Software bugs. The presence of software bugs can be a basis for potential exploitable vulnerabiliti… - Software bugs. The presence of software bugs can be a basis for potential exploitable vulnerabilities. This is particularly true if software has not been tested to verify that known bad code/bugs is not present and reduce the risk of unknown bad code/bugs being present.
- **xid**: x0400000000039340
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Asset_Vulnerability_to_Attack" target="x0400000000039301"/>
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039323"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x0400000000039345">

Using remainders from development (e.g. debug ports, JTAG ports, microprocessors, development certi… - Using remainders from development (e.g. debug ports, JTAG ports, microprocessors, development certificates, developer passwords, …) can permit access to ECUs or permit attackers to gain higher privileges
- **xid**: x0400000000039345
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039323"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x0400000000039349">

Superfluous internet ports left open, providing access to network systems - Superfluous  internet ports left open, providing access to network systems
- **xid**: x0400000000039349
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039327"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Method" id="x040000000003934D">

Circumvent network separation to gain control. Specific example is the use of unprotected gateways,… - Circumvent network separation to gain control. Specific example is the use of unprotected gateways, or access points (such as truck-trailer gateways), to circumvent protections and gain access to other network segments to perform malicious acts, such as sending arbitrary CAN bus messages
- **xid**: x040000000003934D
- **sid**: SI0193
<treqs-link type="Exploits" target="x0400000000039327"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000039351">

Information Exchange - 3.13.1. Guidance related to structured information exchange may be found in ITU-T X.1500 Series for Structured Cybersecurity Information Exchange (CYBEX) Techniques 

3.13.2. The following provides references from the ITU-T X.1500 series may be used for exchanging structured cybersecurity information to enhance cybersecurity through coherent, comprehensive, global, timely and assured information exchange about vulnerabilities, weaknesses, attack patterns and so on:

• X.1520 Common vulnerabilities and exposures (CVE)
• X.1521 Common vulnerability scoring system (CVSS)
• X.1524 Common weakness enumeration (CWE)
• X.1525 Common weakness scoring system (CWSS)
• X.1544 Common attack pattern enumeration and classification (CAPEC)
- **xid**: x0400000000039351
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000039354">

System Software security - acquisition, development and maintenance - 3.10.1. The following points may apply: 
• Secure communications used for updates
• Implement cryptographic protection and signing of software updates
• Implement the use of configuration templates and policies
• Ensure configuration control and that it is possible to roll-back updates
• Version and timestamp and logging of updates
• Ensure the veracity of the update
• Establish secure update procedures, including configuration templates and policies for updates
• For updates, applications should be reviewed and tested to ensure there is no adverse impact on vehicle and organisational security.

3.10.1.1. Points related to “Secure software development”
• Adopt secure coding practices
• Ensure development/debug backdoors are not present in production code
• Ensure that no system errors can be returned to the user/ driver/ HMI
• Ensure all logical decisions have a default clause
• Ensure no development environment kit is contained in the build directories
• Memory management
• Input Validation
• Output Encoding
• Code modification prevention
• Error handling
• Error handling, exception handling and logging
• Ensure that the application fails in a secure manner and redundancy options are available in case of a failure
• Ensure resources are released if an error occurs
• Ensure that no sensitive information is logged in the event of an error
• Search for any calls to the underlying operating system or file open calls and examine the error possibilities
• Ensure application errors are logged
• Apply software testing and integrity checking techniques • Examine the application for debug logging with the view to logging of sensitive data
• Examine the file structure. Are there any components, which should not be directly accessible, available to the user?
• Examine all memory allocations/de-allocations
• Examine the application for dynamic SQL and determine if it is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks
• Search for commented out code, commented out test code, which may contain sensitive information

• Session management
• Examine session invalidation
• Examine how and when a session is created for a user and how it is unauthenticated and authenticated
• Examine the session ID and verify if it is complex enough to fulfil requirements regarding strength
• Determine the actions the application takes if an invalid session ID occurs
• Determine how multithreaded/multi-user session management is performed.
• Determine the session HTTP inactivity timeout
• Determine how the log-out functionality functions

3.10.1.2. Points related to “Secure software testing”
• Testing of security functionality should be carried out during development.
• Acceptance testing programs and related criteria should be established for new systems, upgrades and software versions.”
- **xid**: x0400000000039354
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000039357">

System Design - 3.9.1. The following points may apply:
3.9.1.1. Points related to “Network design”
• Avoid flat networks (apply defence in depth, isolation of components and network segregation)
• Network segmentation and implementation of trust boundaries
• Protections of external internet connections, including authentication/verification of messages received and provision of encrypted communication channels
• Sandboxing for protected execution of 3rd party software
• The use of combinations of gateways, firewalls, intrusion prevention or detection mechanisms, and monitoring are employed to defend systems
• Ensure all internal and external connections (user and entity) go through an appropriate and adequate form of authentication. Be assured that this control cannot be bypassed
• Ensure that authentication credentials do not traverse in clear text form

3.9.1.2. Points related to “Control of data held on vehicles and servers and communicated therefrom”
• General
• Implement appropriate data controls
• Ensure that sensitive information is not compromised
• Apply data minimisation and purpose limitation techniques to reduce the impact should data be lost
• Data minimisation techniques applied to communications
• Systems are designed so that end-users can efficiently and appropriately access, delete and manage their personal data
• Apply techniques to prevent fraudulent manipulation of critical system data
• Apply strict write permissions and authentication measures for updating/ accessing vehicle parameters
• Ensure secure flag is set to prevent accidental transmission in the vehicular network
• Use of cryptography
• A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information is developed and followed. This should include an identification of what data is held and the need to protect it
• Secure storage of sensitive information should be applied
• Encrypt sensitive data and ensure keys are appropriately and securely managed
• Use active memory protection
• Consider the use of Hardware Security Module (HSM), tamper detection, and device authentication techniques to reduce vulnerabilities
• Authentication
• Ensure that whenever authentication credentials or any other sensitive information is passed, only accept the information via secure information protocols and channels through the vehicle communication channel
• Ensure all pages enforce the requirement for authentication for sensitive information
• Cookies
• Determine if all state transitions in the application code properly check for cookies and enforce their use
• Ensure that unauthorized activities cannot take place via cookie manipulation
• Ensure cookies contain as little private (user/driver) information as possible
• Ensure entire cookie is encrypted if sensitive data is persisted in the cookie
• Define all cookies being used by the application, their name, and why they are needed
• Data validation
• Ensure session data is being validated
• Ensure that a data validation mechanism is present
• Ensure all input that can (and will) be modified by a malicious user such as HTTP headers, input fields, hidden fields, drop down lists, and other web components are properly validated
• Ensure that proper length checks on all input exist
• Ensure that all fields, cookies, http headers/bodies, and form fields are validated
• Ensure that data is well formed and contains only known good characters if possible
• Ensure that data validation occurs on the server side
• Examine where data validation occurs and if a centralized model or decentralized model is used
• Ensure there are no backdoors in the data validation model
• Golden Rule: All external input, no matter what it is, is examined and validated


3.9.1.3. Points related to “Controls for messages”

• Only allow a safe set of instructions to be passed to a vehicle
• Message authentication and integrity checking
• Authentication of data
• Verify that the size of received data matches expected values
• Limit and monitor message content and protocols
• Employing rate limiting measures based on context
• Input validation for all messages
• Application based input validation (in terms of what kind of data/input the affected application is expecting)
• Consistency checks using other vehicle sensors (e.g. temperature, radar…)
• Setting acknowledgement messages for V2X messages (currently not standardised)
• Techniques to prevent replay attacks, such as timestamping and use of freshness values
• Timestamping messages and setting expiration time for messages
• Ensure that whenever authentication credentials or any other sensitive information is passed, only accept the information via the HTTP “POST” method and will not accept it via the HTTP “GET” method
• Any page deemed by the business or the development team as being outside the scope of authentication should be reviewed in order to assess any possibility of security breach
- **xid**: x0400000000039357
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x040000000003935A">

Physical and environmental security - 3.7.1. No further points identified.
- **xid**: x040000000003935A
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x040000000003935D">

Cryptographic security - 3.6.1. The following points may apply:

3.6.1.1. Points related to “Cryptographic key management”
• Actively manage and protect cryptographic keys
• Effective key management and protection for any cryptography used

3.6.1.2. Points related to “Encryption of communication and software”
• Encryption for communications containing sensitive data, including software updates
• Encryption of software code
• Ensure no sensitive data is transmitted in clear text, internally or externally
• Ensure the application is implementing known good cryptographic methods
- **xid**: x040000000003935D
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000039368">

Supplier relationships security - 3.11.2. The points may also apply: 
• Cyber security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with supplier’s products/ system to the manufacturers products/system shall be agreed with the supplier and documented
• All relevant cyber security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier that may access, process, store, communicate, or provide infrastructure components for, the manufacturers
• Agreements with suppliers shall include requirements to address the cyber security risks associated with information and communications technology services and product supply chain
• Manufacturer shall regularly monitor, review and audit supplier service delivery
• Changes to the provision of services by suppliers, including maintaining and improving existing cyber security policies, procedures and controls, shall be managed, taking account of the criticality of business information, systems, components and processes involved and re-assessment of risks
- **xid**: x0400000000039368
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Standard_Process_Requirement" id="x0400000000039370">

Organizational security - 3.2.1. The following points may apply: 

• Cyber security roles and responsibilities to be defined and allocated
• Segregation of duties to reduce opportunities for unauthorized/ unintentional modification/misuse of organization’s assets
• Appropriate contact with relevant authorities shall be made for activities like security incident management
• Contact with special interest groups, specialist security forums and professional associations shall be maintained for effective cybersecurity knowledge management
- **xid**: x0400000000039370
- **sid**: SI0236

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x0400000000039375">

Perform cryptographic key verification - If a key authentication request is received, then ImobMgr perform cryptographic verification and securely exhange data with Fob and Engine Control Module (ECM).
- **xid**: x0400000000039375
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Design_Requirement" id="x040000000003937E">

Handle alarm request - If a security breach or unauthorized start attempt is detected, then the ImobMgr shall initiate an alarm request to the vehicle's alert systems.
- **xid**: x040000000003937E
- **sid**: SI0125

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Abstract_Interface" id="x040000000003938F">

Remote operation
- **xid**: x040000000003938F
- **sid**: SI0230

</treqs-element>

OBD cable

Information can be readily disclosed. For example through eavesdropping on communications or throug… - Information can be readily disclosed. For example through eavesdropping on communications or through allowing unauthorized access to sensitive files or folders

Messages received by the vehicle (for example X2V or diagnostic messages), or transmitted within it… - Messages received by the vehicle (for example X2V or diagnostic messages), or transmitted within it, contain malicious content

LIN - Local Interconnect Network according to ISO 17987.

Automotive Ethernet

Interception of information / interfering radiations / monitoring communications - Interception of information / interfering radiations / monitoring communications
- **xid**: x04000000000393AB

• Avoid flat networks (apply defence in depth, isolation of components and network segregation)

• Protections of external internet connections, including authentication/verification of messages received and provision of encrypted communication channels

• Ensure that sensitive information is not compromised
• Apply data minimisation and purpose limitation techniques to reduce the impact should data be lost
• Data minimisation techniques applied to communications
• Systems are designed so that end-users can efficiently and appropriately access, delete and manage their personal data

• Verify that the size of received data matches expected values

• Timestamping messages and setting expiration time for messages

Malicious internal (e.g. CAN) messages - Malicious internal (e.g. CAN) messages

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Requirement" id="x04000000000393F9">

Verify received data is sent from valid entity - The Navigation ECU shall authenticate the transmitter of navigation data, according to the approved methods defined in reference [14].
- **xid**: x04000000000393F9
- **sid**: SI0187

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Vector" id="x040000000003940B">

An attacker compromise Navigation ECU from OBD-II connector - Since the authentication code is essentially non-existent, an attacker can deliver malicious payload through OBD-II connector interface compromising the integrity of the Gateway ECU enabling the injection of spoofed lamp switch off requests.
- **xid**: x040000000003940B
- **sid**: SI0202
<treqs-link type="On_Component" target="x04000000000393E7"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Vector" id="x0400000000039410">

An attacker compromise Navigation ECU from cellular interface - Since the authentication code is essentially non-existent, an attacker can deliver malicious payload through Cellular interface compromising the integrity of the Gateway ECU enabling the injection of spoofed lamp switch off requests.
- **xid**: x0400000000039410
- **sid**: SI0202
<treqs-link type="On_Component" target="x04000000000393F5"/>
<treqs-link type="Control" target="x04000000000393F9"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Attack_Vector" id="x0400000000039415">

An attacker compromise Navigation ECU from bluetooth - Since the authentication code is essentially non-existent, an attacker can deliver malicious payload through Bluetooth interface compromising the integrity of the Gateway ECU enabling the injection of spoofed lamp switch off requests.
- **xid**: x0400000000039415
- **sid**: SI0202
<treqs-link type="On_Component" target="x0400000000039404"/>

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Requirement" id="x0400000000039425">

Make the CAN channel difficult to get access to - Make the CAN channel physically difficult to access.
- **xid**: x0400000000039425
- **sid**: SI0187

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Requirement" id="x0400000000039430">

Authenctication - Can be done certificate based, token based, biometric based, multi factor, password etc..
Therefore the attribut ratings are imaginary (let's assume certificate based)
- **xid**: x0400000000039430
- **sid**: SI0187

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Cybersecurity_Goal" id="x0400000000039448">

Authentication between key fob and antenna should be securly established. - To avaiod spoofing attacks a secure method of authentication should be in place.
- **xid**: x0400000000039448
- **sid**: SI0166

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x040000000003947B">

Prevention of unauthorized Engine start - The immobilizer system shall only allow engine start when a valid transponder key or key fob is detected and authenticated.
- **xid**: x040000000003947B
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x0400000000039480">

Prevention of unintended Engine shutdown - The immobilizer ECU must allow the engine to remain operational once the vehicle is in motion, even if authentication is lost.
- **xid**: x0400000000039480
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x0400000000039484">

Detection and recovery from communication failures - The immobilizer shall attempt automatic recovery from communication failures by restarting the authentication process up to three times before entering fail-safe mode.
- **xid**: x0400000000039484
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x0400000000039488">

Protection against cybersecurity threats - All communication between the immobilizer ECU and the key fob must be AES-128 or higher encrypted to prevent unauthorized access.
- **xid**: x0400000000039488
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x040000000003948C">

Fault tolerent and Fail safe system - In case of immobilizer ECU failure, the system shall allow a manual override using a secondary authentication method (e.g., emergency PIN entry).
- **xid**: x040000000003948C
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Functional_safety_requirement" id="x0400000000039490">

Electrical power safety requirement - Immobilzer shall operate in Voltage range of 9V to 16V, and consume maximum standby current of <1mA when the vehicle is off to prevent battery drain. It shall detect over and under voltage.
- **xid**: x0400000000039490
- **sid**: SI0375

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x040000000003950C">

UN Regulation No.97 (ECE R97) - The vehicle shall comply with United Nations regulations applicable to anti-theft and immobilizer systems.
- **xid**: x040000000003950C
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Legal_Requirement" id="x0400000000039511">

FMVSS No.114 - The vehicle shall prevent engine start without key authentication and shall secue against unauthorized movement.
- **xid**: x0400000000039511
- **sid**: SI0462

</treqs-element>

<treqs-element type="Function_Requirement" id="x040000000003A013">

Engine start/stop - The immobilizer system shall control the engine start and stop functionality based on authentication of a valid key. If valid key is detected, the system shall allow engine to start. If the key is unauthorized or missing, the system shall prevent start to ensure vehicle security.
- **xid**: x040000000003A013
- **sid**: SI0011

</treqs-element>
